operating_state: Total revenue increased 28% sequentially to $10.7 billion driven by higher crude oil, NGL and natural gas prices across all segments. Oil and gas production increased 76 Mboe/d to 1,150 Mboe/d as Al Hosn Gas and Algeria returned online from scheduled maintenance. Chemical operations benefited from higher realized pricing and volumes while midstream captured improved commodity spreads and higher sulfur prices.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased from higher downhole maintenance, energy costs and purchased injectant costs in the Permian Basin plus elevated Gulf of Mexico surface operations. Chemical segment faced higher raw material costs partially offsetting improved pricing. Depreciation, depletion and amortization decreased year-over-year due to lower per-barrel rates from higher proved reserves added during 2021. Interest expense declined significantly following aggressive debt reduction.
material_events: Occidental repaid $8.1 billion face value of debt across Q1 and Q2 2022, using cash and recognizing $161 million gain on early extinguishment. The company declared common dividends and repurchased 12.4 million shares during the first half. Management completed a legal entity reorganization in Q1 generating a $2.6 billion non-cash tax benefit and entered a new 25-year production sharing arrangement with Algeria partners in July 2022.
macro_exposures: Operations remain highly dependent on oil price volatility with WTI averaging $101.35 for the six-month period versus $61.96 in prior year. The Russia-Ukraine war continues impacting global commodity markets though Occidental operates no assets in either country. Chemical segment margins face pressure from supply chain interruptions, labor constraints and rising inflation affecting housing, construction, automotive and durable goods markets.
forward_outlook: Management prioritizes maximizing cash flow while sustaining 2021 production levels and maintaining capital discipline. Operating cash flows will fund continued debt reduction, robust liquidity maintenance, and shareholder returns through sustainable dividends and active share buybacks.
uncertainty_profile: Zero Coupon bonds totaling $415 million can be put to Occidental in October 2022 requiring potential cash payment. Interest rate swaps with $308 million fair value face mandatory termination in September 2022 and 2023 with settlement amounts fluctuating based on rate movements. Environmental remediation obligations continue under applicable laws with retroactive liability regardless of fault.
